title: श्री गायत्री स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गायत्री स्तोत्रम् ॥

नमस्ते देवि गायत्रि सावित्रि त्रिपदेऽक्षरे।
अजरे अमरे माता त्राहि मां भवसागरात्॥ १॥

नमस्ते सूर्यसङ्काशे सूर्यकोटिसमप्रभे।
ब्रह्मविष्णुशिवानन्दे ब्रह्मविद्ये नमोऽस्तु ते॥ २॥

नमस्ते वेदजननि नमस्ते ब्रह्मरूपिणि।
नमस्ते ब्रह्मविद्यासि सदा शान्तिप्रदायिनि॥ ३॥

ॐकाररूपिणी देवि, पञ्चप्राणस्वरूपिणी।
पञ्चभूतात्मिके माता, पञ्चकोशविलासिनी॥ ४॥

त्वमेव सन्ध्या सावित्री, त्वमेव जगदम्बिका।
त्वमेव वेदजननी, त्वमेव योगदायिनी॥ ५॥

सदाचार प्रिये देवि, सर्वस्वरूपे शोभने।
त्वां विना जगतां नाथा, न किञ्चिदपि विद्यते॥ ६॥

सृष्टिस्थितिलयानां च, कारणे कारणे सदा।
ब्रह्मज्ञानप्रदे देवि, गायत्रि प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ७॥

त्वमेव मूलप्रकृतिः, त्वमेव परमेश्वरी।
त्वय्येव सर्वं प्रतिष्ठितं, निर्गुणे सगुणेऽमले॥ ८॥

ऋग्वेदगायत्री त्वं हि, यजुर्वेदस्य सावित्री।
सामवेदस्य सरस्वती, अथर्ववेदमहेश्वरी॥ ९॥

प्रातःकाले तु बालार्का, मध्याह्ने तु युवत्यसि।
सायं काले तु वृद्धा त्वं, त्रिसन्ध्ये त्रिस्वरूपिणी॥ १०॥

गायत्री स्तोत्रमिदं पुण्यं, यः पठेत् भक्तिसंयुतः।
सर्वपापैः विमुच्येत, ब्रह्मलोकं स गच्छति॥ ११॥

॥ इति श्री गायत्री स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Gayatri Stotram ||

Namaste Devi Gayatri Savitri tripade-akshare |
Ajare amare Mata trahi mam bhavasagarat || 1 ||

Namaste Surya-sankashe Surya-koti-samaprabhe |
Brahma-Vishnu-Shivanande Brahmavidye namo-astu te || 2 ||

Namaste Veda-janani namaste Brahma-rupini |
Namaste Brahmavidyasi sada shanti-pradayini || 3 ||

Omkara-rupini Devi, pancha-prana-svarupini |
Pancha-bhutatmike Mata, pancha-kosha-vilasini || 4 ||

Tvameva Sandhya Savitri, tvameva Jagadambika |
Tvameva Veda-janani, tvameva Yoga-dayini || 5 ||

Sadachara priye Devi, sarva-svarupe Shobhane |
Tvam vina jagatam natha, na kinchid-api vidyate || 6 ||

Srishti-sthiti-layanam cha, karane karane sada |
Brahma-jnana-prade Devi, Gayatri pranamamy-aham || 7 ||

Tvameva mula-prakritih, tvameva Parameshwari |
Tvayy-eva sarvam pratishthitam, nirgune sagune-amale || 8 ||

Rigveda-Gayatri tvam hi, Yajurvedasya Savitri |
Samavedasya Saraswati, Atharvaveda-Maheshwari || 9 ||

Pratah-kale tu bala-arka, madhyahne tu yuvatyasi |
Sayam kale tu vriddha tvam, trisandhye tri-svarupini || 10 ||

Gayatri stotram-idam punyam, yah pathet bhakti-samyutah |
Sarva-papaih vimuchyeta, Brahmalokam sa gachchhati || 11 ||

|| Iti Shri Gayatri Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: हे गायत्री देवी, सावित्री, त्रिपदा अक्षरा, अजर-अमर माता, आपको नमस्कार! मुझे भवसागर से बचाइए। करोड़ सूर्यों के समान प्रभा वाली, ब्रह्मा-विष्णु-शिव को आनन्द देने वाली, ब्रह्मविद्या स्वरूपा, वेद-जननी, ब्रह्मरूपिणी, सदा शान्ति प्रदान करने वाली, ओंकार स्वरूपा, पञ्चप्राणमयी, पञ्चभूतात्मिका — आप ही सन्ध्या, सावित्री, जगदम्बिका, वेद-जननी और योगदायिनी हैं। आप ऋग्वेद में गायत्री, यजुर्वेद में सावित्री, सामवेद में सरस्वती और अथर्ववेद में महेश्वरी हैं। प्रातःकाल बालिका, मध्याह्न में युवती और सायंकाल वृद्धा — तीन सन्ध्याओं में तीन स्वरूपा हैं।
benefits: Invokes the complete grace of Gayatri Mata
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and purifies the soul
Invokes the blessing of peace and spiritual illumination
Protects the devotee from the cycle of rebirth
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Gayatri, Gayatri Stotram (श्री गायत्री स्तोत्रम्) stands as a powerful Vedic meditation mantra. Attributed to Devi Bhagavatam, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to protect the devotee from the cycle of rebirth and destroy all sins and purify the soul. It is also said to lead to the supreme abode (Brahmaloka). It is especially recommended on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Gayatri Jayanti for maximum spiritual benefit. The text contains 11 verses and takes around 8 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Gayatri Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Gayatri Stotram in Sanskrit with English transliteration and Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn praising Gayatri Mata, mother of all Vedas.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Gayatri Stotram and which deity does it praise?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gayatri Stotram is an eleven-verse Sanskrit hymn from the Devi Bhagavatam praising Goddess Gayatri, the personified deity of the supreme Vedic Gayatri Mantra (Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah Tat Savitur Varenyam Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat). The hymn salutes her as Tripade-akshare (the three-footed syllabic deity), Ajare-amare (unaging, undying), Surya-koti-sama-prabhe (radiant as crores of suns), Brahmavidye (the supreme Brahma-knowledge), Veda-janani (mother of the Vedas), Omkara-rupini (form of Om), and the threefold Sandhya/Savitri/Saraswati who manifests differently in the three Vedas — Gayatri in Rig, Savitri in Yajur, Saraswati in Sama, and Maheshwari in Atharva. (Sources: Devi Bhagavatam; Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia Gayatri.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Goddess Gayatri and what is her significance?
seoFaqs.answer: Goddess Gayatri is the personified deity of the supreme Vedic Gayatri Mantra and is worshipped as Veda-mata — the mother of all four Vedas. She is the Brahma-vidya itself in feminine form and represents the cosmic intelligence (chit-shakti) that illumines the buddhi (intellect). Iconographically she is five-faced (representing pancha-prana, panchabhuta, panchakosha) and ten-armed (representing the ten directions and ten forms of energy). The stotra describes her as appearing differently at three sandhyas: Bala-arka (young sun) form at dawn (Brahmi/Gayatri), Yuvati (youthful) form at noon (Vaishnavi/Savitri), and Vriddha (aged) form at sunset (Rudri/Saraswati). She is the consort of Brahma in some traditions and the supreme Adi-Shakti in others. (Sources: Devi Bhagavatam; Hindupedia Gayatri; Pandit Shriram Sharma Acharya.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Gayatri Mantra and its meaning?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gayatri Mantra is the supreme Vedic mantra revealed to sage Vishvamitra in Rig Veda 3.62.10. It has 24 syllables in three padas (hence Tripada) plus the introductory pranava and three vyahritis. The full mantra is: Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah / Tat Savitur Varenyam / Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi / Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat. It means: 'We meditate on the supreme luminous Savitur (Sun-deity) of the three worlds (earth, mid-region, heaven); may that divine effulgence illuminate our intellects (dhi).' The mantra is the foundational sandhya-vandana mantra of every dvija (twice-born), recited a minimum of 10/27/108 times at three sandhyas daily — pratah, madhyahna and sayam — and is the seed of all subsequent Hindu devotional traditions. (Sources: Rig Veda 3.62.10; Hindupedia Gayatri Mantra; Sandhya Vandana Bhashya.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should Gayatri Stotram be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Sunday (Ravivar) is the prescribed weekday since Gayatri is the Shakti of Surya. The supreme occasion is Gayatri Jayanti — Jyestha Shukla Ekadashi (May-June, the day after Nirjala Ekadashi) when Vishvamitra received the mantra. Daily recitation should ideally happen at the three sandhyas: brahma-muhurta/dawn, noon, and sunset — the same windows as sandhya-vandana. The stotra is chanted facing east at dawn, north at noon, west at sunset. Devotees use a tulsi or rudraksha mala for 108 repetitions of the Gayatri Mantra interspersed with stotra recitation. The Gayatri Pariwar (founded by Pandit Shriram Sharma Acharya at Shantikunj, Haridwar) holds annual sankha Gayatri yajnas with collective stotra-paatha. (Sources: Vedic sandhya tradition; Drikpanchang Gayatri Jayanti; Gayatri Pariwar.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the three forms of Gayatri at three sandhyas?
seoFaqs.answer: The tenth verse of the stotra describes Gayatri's three forms at the three sandhyas (junctions of day): Pratah-kale tu Bala-arka (at dawn she is Bala-arka — the young rising-sun form, Brahmi shakti, white-complexioned, riding the hamsa, granting srishti-creativity); Madhyahne tu Yuvatyasi (at noon she is Yuvati — the youthful form, Vaishnavi shakti, red-complexioned, riding the Garuda, granting sthiti-sustenance); Sayam-kale tu Vriddha tvam (at sunset she is Vriddha — the elderly form, Rudri shakti, dark-complexioned, riding the Vrishabha, granting laya-dissolution). This trisandhye tri-svarupini doctrine corresponds to the three Vedic deities — Gayatri, Savitri, Saraswati — and to the three guna and the three lokas. (Sources: Devi Bhagavatam 12.6; Hindupedia Gayatri trisandhya; Sandhya Bhashya.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Gayatri Stotram grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti declares that one who recites this punya stotra with bhakti is freed from all sins (sarva-papaih vimuchyeta) and attains Brahmaloka (Brahmalokam sa gachchhati). Specific benefits include awakening of buddhi (the intellect Gayatri specifically illuminates), success in education and Vedic study, protection from cycles of rebirth, peace and spiritual illumination, the combined grace of Saraswati (knowledge), Lakshmi (prosperity) and Durga (protection) — since Gayatri is regarded as their root-shakti — purification of speech and thought, attainment of brahma-jnana, and finally moksha through identification with the cosmic intelligence. The Vasishtha Smriti declares Gayatri-japa-paatha purifies even the maha-pataki. (Sources: Devi Bhagavatam phalashruti; Vasishtha Smriti; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Gayatri Stotram differ from Gayatri Mantra and Gayatri Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: These three Gayatri compositions serve different functions. The Gayatri Mantra (24 syllables, Rig Veda 3.62.10) is the supreme Vedic seed-mantra recited as japa (1, 10, 27, 108, 1008 times) at three sandhyas — it is mantra, not stotra. The Gayatri Stotram (11 verses, Devi Bhagavatam) is the personified praise hymn used as stuti during puja and meditation — it praises the deity behind the mantra. The Gayatri Sahasranama (1000 names, Devi Bhagavatam Skanda 12) is the comprehensive archana-paatha used by Sanskrit-trained upasakas during full Gayatri puja — typically 20-25 minutes. Daily sadhana usually combines all three: mantra-japa for transmission of energy, stotra for personalised devotion, sahasranama for archana on Gayatri Jayanti and Sundays. (Sources: Hindupedia; Gayatri Pariwar texts; Stotra Nidhi.)
